grounding_note: Expansion batch: more capacity, exploitation, and instrument-scope disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: My uncle keeps giving money to a 'girlfriend' online that none of us have ever met.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's no insider to sue — it's an overseas romance scam, and the real question is a conservatorship: his doctor confirms his judgment is impaired and he won't stop.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's an overseas romance scam — nobody local to sue. His doctor confirms impaired judgment, and he won't stop; we need a conservatorship.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a reachable exploiter exists or protective appointment is the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'girlfriend' turned out to be his own caregiver's daughter running the account from ten minutes away — a reachable exploiter with his money.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The 'girlfriend' is his caregiver's daughter running the account locally — she has his money.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scam-vs-insider distinction determines whether there's a defendant or only protection.

scenarios.opening_query: Dad gave my brother control of everything years ago and now Dad wants some of it back.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'control' is just a power of attorney — Dad is fully competent, and revoking or rewriting the POA is a simple document matter he can sign tomorrow.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's just a power of attorney — Dad's fully competent and can revoke or rewrite it tomorrow.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what instrument granted control and whether the elder retains capacity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a court-ordered conservatorship from his stroke years — undoing it means a restoration proceeding with medical proof.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a court conservatorship from his stroke years — undoing it takes a restoration case with medical proof.
scenarios.vetting_note: Instrument identity (POA vs court order) sets the difficulty and route.

scenarios.opening_query: The assisted-living place says my mother can't stay unless the family signs new financial guarantees.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Long-term Care Issues || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: Nobody holds any authority — Mom's still sharp, and the real fix is her signing financial and healthcare POAs so one of us can lawfully deal with the facility.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nobody has authority — Mom's still sharp, and the fix is her signing POAs so one of us can lawfully deal with the facility.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether authority documents exist and the elder can execute them
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The documents exist — the fight is the facility's demand itself, which our ombudsman says violates the resident-rights rules on third-party guarantees.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We have the documents — the fight is their guarantee demand, which the ombudsman says violates resident-rights rules.
scenarios.vetting_note: Missing-documents vs unlawful-demand postures at the same facility.

scenarios.opening_query: My grandmother's new 'best friend' drove her to a lawyer's office last month and now everything is different.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Grandma passed two weeks ago — the 'new' will from that visit leaves the friend nearly everything, and contesting it in probate is now the only path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Grandma passed two weeks ago — that visit's 'new' will gives the friend nearly everything, so it's a will contest now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the elder is living (protective action) or deceased (will contest)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Grandma's alive — and the friend is now blocking our calls and visits while 'managing' her accounts; we need protective action immediately.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's alive — and the friend now blocks our calls while 'managing' her accounts. We need protection now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Alive/deceased fact flips urgency and forum entirely.

scenarios.opening_query: My disabled brother's group home controls his money and won't account for any of it.
scenarios.initial: Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.final: Wills & Trusts > Special Needs/Inc. Cap Trusts
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments || Wills & Trusts > Special Needs/Inc. Cap Trusts
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bigger event: he's about to inherit $150,000 from our father — it needs a special-needs trust immediately or he loses his SSI and Medicaid, and the group home can't be near it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's inheriting $150,000 from Dad — it needs a special-needs trust now or his SSI and Medicaid are gone, and the group home can't touch it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an imminent inheritance requires benefit-protective trust work
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Wills & Trusts > Reversing Guardianship or Payee Assignments
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No inheritance — the issue is the payee arrangement itself, and we want it moved from the facility to family with a full accounting.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No inheritance — it's the payee arrangement we want moved to family, with an accounting.
scenarios.vetting_note: Inheritance-timing fact selects trust creation over payee reversal.

scenarios.opening_query: We need a guardianship for my mother-in-law before she signs anything else her nephew puts in front of her.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's past prevention — he already had her deed him the rental duplex and add him to two accounts; clawing those back is the case, with any guardianship second.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's past prevention — he already got the duplex deeded over and himself onto two accounts. Clawing that back is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether completed transfers need recovery beyond protective appointment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's been taken yet — the doctors say she can't manage decisions, and the appointment itself is the protection we need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's taken yet — doctors say she can't manage decisions, so the appointment is the protection.
scenarios.vetting_note: Completed-transfer facts make recovery the case; row 56's protective side otherwise.
